channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Plans for a Trump family-linked resort spark protests in Albania
item.description: A coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner is facing resistance in Albania. The government says the project will transform the nation, but environmental campaigners and critics oppose it.
item.content:encoded:
A coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner is facing resistance in Albania. The government says the project will transform the nation, but environmental campaigners and critics oppose it.

(Image credit: Hameraldi Agolli)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy,' says they still get along
item.description: The president also acknowledged that he cursed at the Israeli leader in a heated phone call in which he told Benjamin Netanyahu not to bomb the Lebanese capital Beirut.
item.content:encoded:
The president also acknowledged that he cursed at the Israeli leader in a heated phone call in which he told Benjamin Netanyahu not to bomb the Lebanese capital Beirut.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Greetings from Porto, whose lanes are lined with colorful textiles
item.description: Linen scarves, cotton aprons and dishtowels adorn the entrances to souvenir shops, many of which are run by Bangladeshis whose home country shares Portugal's rich tradition of textile manufacturing.
item.content:encoded: Linen scarves, cotton aprons and dishtowels adorn the entrances to souvenir shops, many of which are run by Bangladeshis whose home country shares Portugal's rich tradition of textile manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Esme Nicholson

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike a St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin visit
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as the city hosted an annual economic forum promoted by President Putin.
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as the city hosted an annual economic forum promoted by President Putin.

(Image credit: AP Photo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Kuwait says Iranian drones hit airport and killed 1 as ceasefire is tested again
item.description: Kuwait says Iranian drones have heavily damaged a passenger terminal at its main airport, killing one person, wounding dozens and briefly closing the airfield. Iran denies damaging the airport.
item.content:encoded:
Kuwait says Iranian drones have heavily damaged a passenger terminal at its main airport, killing one person, wounding dozens and briefly closing the airfield. Iran denies damaging the airport.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Scenes from the aftermath of Russia's latest massive attack on Kyiv
item.description: Russian missile and drone attacks killed civilians across Ukraine overnight, in one of Moscow's largest assaults in recent months.
item.content:encoded:
Russian missile and drone attacks killed civilians across Ukraine overnight, in one of Moscow's largest assaults in recent months.

(Image credit: Anton Shtuka for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Polina Lytvynova

item.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
item.description: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.

(Image credit: Jean-Francois Badias)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink
item.description: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
item.content:encoded: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
item.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.
item.content:encoded:
Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What to know about U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats
item.description: The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
